english: adj. 1. quick, swift Lex., kro- (khro-) or sdaN-skyen-pa (sdang skyen pa) quick to wrath Stg.; byed-skyen-pa (byed skyen pa) rash, hasty, precipitate Glr. - 2. nimble, dexterous C.W.

bottle, flask; the water-flask of the hukka; bottle-shaped ornaments in architecture, e.g. on the cenotaphs or Chodtén; rdzá-mai (rdza mai) earthen-bottle, pitcher; śél-bum (shel bum) glass-bottle; čáṅ-bum (chang bum) beer-bottle; mčód-bum (mchod bum) Cs. vessel used in sacrificing; mé-bum (me bum) cupping-glass Lt. (cf. puṅ-pa (pung pa)).
1. bolt, bar, vertically fastened to a door etc., t'óg-bur (thog bur) upper, yóg-bur (yog bur) lower bolt. - 2. for bu-rám (bu ram). - 3. for °bur ('bur).
(or perh. tiṅ (ting)) Sch., a kind of bell or gong in temples.
n. of certain plants in Ld. & Kun.

W., *bhul, bhụl, bhṳ and bhṳ-tog* (bhul, bhul, bhu and bhu tog) C., Med., (the spellings of Campbell, peu - v. bá-mo (ba mo) - and of Schl., phuli, have prob. resulted from a mistake in hearing), soda, not unfrequently found in Tibet as a white powder on the ground, and used as a medicine, as a ferment, as a means for giving additional flavour to tea, and for various technical purposes.
slow, heavy, tardy, gro búl-te (gro bul te) slow in walking, making but tardy progress Dzl.; W.: *dúl-če búl-po* (*dul ce bul po*).
*bul-gar* (bul gar) W., Russia leather, jufts.
1. for byís-pa (byis pa) Lt. - 2. v. °búd-pa ('bud pa).
1. num. figure: 105. - 2. W. for bye (bye). - 3. for words here not noted refer to pe (pe).
S.g.?
v. beg-gé (beg ge).
also -tsoṅ (-tsong), Ssk. गदा, 1. club, with an ornamental knob, prob. merely an attribute of gods. - 2. n. of a goddess Thgr.
a geographical prop. name, prob. = Himalaya, Pth.
vulg. calf.
v. °bé-dha ('be dha).
Cs., thick slime or mucus, e.g. the mucus flowing at childbirth from the vagina Lt.
also beu-búm (be'u bum), writing, scripture, book Glr., perh. the same word as the following.
are stated to be the sacred writings of the Bonpos, which - as our Lama candidly owned - 'are also perused by Buddhists for their edification'.
cow-calf, female calf C.
Nakshatra, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) 3.
W., from the Hind. ब्याज, interest, ṭá-ka bé-za (ta ka be za) a double paisa interest, of 1 rupee, = 4-6 pCt. pro month.
(spelling?), fillet of the women in Ld., ornamented with coloured stones.
S.g., a kind of surgical instrument.
Sch. great-grandfather.
oak-tree, = ča-ra (ca ra); be-k'ród (be khrod) oak-forest Wdn.
Lt., a disease; Sch.: measles.
a hidden shirt of mail.
Sch., stick, cudgel, club.

1. = ke (ke), ke-béd (ke bed), advantage, profit, gain, high price, tsá-la drúg-ču bed yód-pai skábs-su (tsa la drug cu bed yod pai skabs su) at a time when salt was a sixty times dearer (than barley) Glr.; *bed tób-če* (*bed tob ce*) W. to gain, to make profit; bed-čód (bed chod) Mil. is stated to be the same as loṅs-spyód (longs spyod); bed-čód tsod bčád-de (bed chod tsod bcad de) to be temperate, to keep moderation in the indulgence of the appetites. - 2. interest, C., W.
a large pitcher; jug, beer-pot, Glr.; *ču-bhén* (*chu bhen*), water-pot, C.

1. dead matter, mostly applied to the body, as opp. to the soul, rig-pa (rig pa), e.g. bem rig gye-dus (bem rig gye dus) when body and soul are parting, Mil., Thgr.; lus bem-rig gnyís-kyi so-mtsáms-su (lus bem rig gnyis kyi so mtshams su) on the
